background: Trees were surveyed for the presence of lichen. Each lichen found was recorded as either occurring on the north, east, south or west quadrant of the tree: 36 lichens were found on north-facing quadrants, 50 on east-facing quadrants, 29 on south-facing quadrants and 45 on west-facing quadrants.

task.query: For lichens occurring on the north, east, south and west sides of trees, use the external hypothesis that lichen orientation is random, and display in a table the observed and expected number of lichens and the component $X^2$ values in a goodness of fit test.
task.answer:
Observed and expected values along with $X^2$ components are:

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline & Observed & Expected & $X^2$ \\
\hline North & 36 & 40 & 0.4 \\
\hline East & 50 & 40 & 2.5 \\
\hline South & 29 & 40 & 3.025 \\
\hline West & 45 & 40 & 0.625 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

task.query: Calculate the total $X^2, \mathrm{df}$ and $P$ values to complete the goodness of fit test.
What can you conclude about the distribution of lichens on trees?
task.answer: There is no significant departure from random orientation in lichens.

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
